MeshCore
📚 Forums / software-development / meshcore-bot
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-10-18 9:37 p.m.
Hi, I figured I should start a thread for my meshcore-bot project, built with meshcore_py. This started as a casual project for our local mesh and has rapidly become popular with people on Pugetmesh/Salishmesh who want an acknowledgement of their test. A couple key commands are test, which responds with an acknowledgement, path, and final SNR/RSSI, and time. ack [user] | 6d,7e,5f | SNR: 1.5 dB | RSSI: -92 dBm | Received: 18:19:27 Path returns the path with repeater names included (from either the local contacts database or a prefix api from sites like map.w0z.is) Other commands include a channels list, prefix lookup dice/roll, hf conditions, ping/pong, solar conditions, bot stats, sun/moon status, weather (currently NOAA dependent), sports, jokes. Commands are built as plugins and can be developed as self-contained files. https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot
GitHub - agessaman/meshcore-bot
Contribute to agessaman/meshcore-bot development by creating an account on GitHub.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-11-21 11:08 p.m.
I've been working on enabling internationalization in the bot. I've extracted most of the command-related strings and I've created some (probably wildly unnatural) translations using computer translations. I've also added an alternative Weather command that uses OpenMeteo and has coverage for the whole world. I've also added command overrides. If you want to make modifications to a command, you can duplicate the file into ./modules/commands/alternatives and they will override existing commands with the same name field. If you change name = "gwx" to name = "wx" the example global weather command will unload the standard wx command in favor of your replacement. I added the option to use the path guessing logic to estimate path lengths in test messages. New placeholders are {path_distance} for the estimated total path traveled, and {firstlast_distance} for the distance between the first and last known repeater. I also fixed an issue with the packet stream not working in the web viewer.
3
MrAlders0n 2025-11-23 8:13 a.m.
@[PNW] HerculesMulligan I love this, I have been using HA to do this and to be-honest it’s not perfect for a bot. I was wondering if I could toss out a feature request, in HA today I keep track of companion names and the first time they send a message in public, and when they do I respond with the above. We have a lot of people join the mesh before our discord/see our site. It helps guide new users to our discord and by responding quickly it’s a better chance they don’t leave range / disconnect their device or what not

@[PNW] HerculesMulligan I love this, I have been using HA to do this and to be-honest it’s not perfect for a bot. Hmm, how can you command the MeshCore Companion to send a channel message with x hops? I've looked into "Companion Radio Protocol" and couldn't find a parameter. IMHO it's a setting on the Repeaters. Or did I miss something?
Nemo 🇨🇭
Hmm, how can you command the MeshCore Companion to send a channel message with x hops? I've looked into "Companion Radio Protocol" and couldn't find a parameter. IMHO it's a setting on the Repeaters. Or did I miss something?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-07 10:18 a.m.
In this case, the message will still flood normally but the bot parses the path and stores the rx path along with other data. I can use that to only greet path lengths less than x.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-07 6:44 p.m.
Okay, this is getting a little more extensive than I had planned, but it's turning out cool so I'm going to keep going. I have it working with bot RSS feeds and REST APIs. The config process is definitely not for general consumption, but I have it pulling in data from the local department of transportation and outputting them to a channel. It handles deduplication and some rate limiting. If you want to give it a spin, you can clone the dev branch.

12:53 a.m.

Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-28 11:24 p.m.
So the sports command is supposed to pull specific teams information based on what I put in the .ini or does it just pull the latest games?
11:25 p.m.

Kepler - YQLMesh
So the sports command is supposed to pull specific teams information based on what I put in the .ini or does it just pull the latest games?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-28 11:28 p.m.
The config sets the teams that are included in a default run sports in Seattle gets all the local teams. It prioritizes currently ongoing games, results from earlier in the same day, then the next scheduled games.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 12:09 a.m.
I'm going to need to find another source for WHL if we want live scores. I have schedules and past scores working, but they want 9 bucks a month for the v2 API with live scores.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 12:20 a.m.

12:21 a.m.
Just another question, if I want Canadian weather working, do I need to do anything special?
12:21 a.m.
Or are weather and alert US only right now
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 12:23 a.m.
There's an OpenMeteo option available. If you go into modules/commands/alternatives and edit wx_international.py, change this line: keywords = ['gwx', 'globalweather', 'gwxa'] to read: keywords = ['wx', 'gwx', 'globalweather', 'gwxa'] It will override the default NOAA weather.
12:23 a.m.
I need to add an option to the weather command to allow easy switching between them.
12:24 a.m.
Basically, if an alternative command exists and it claims a keyword, the default command for that keyword doesn't load.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 12:26 a.m.
Awesome. Anything else other than changing that line?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 12:26 a.m.
Nope, just restart the bot.

Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:06 a.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:27 a.m.
The settings toggle will be in the next version. It's already in the dev branch if you want to pull that.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:33 a.m.
Cool I pulled dev. I'll take a look to see how to swap
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:35 a.m.
Add this under [Weather] in your config.ini (it's in the example): # Weather provider selection # Options: noaa, openmeteo # noaa: Use NOAA API (US-focused, better for US locations, includes weather alerts) # openmeteo: Use Open-Meteo API (global coverage, works worldwide) # Default: noaa weather_provider = openmeteo
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:35 a.m.
Probably a bunch of other ini changes? Should I grab the dev example and redo the whole ini?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:44 a.m.
There are a couple of settings blocks that were changed, but they're all the way at the bottom. I've been working on service modules, because people wanted one radio to also act as a Map Updater for the official map, or an observer for the analyzer.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:49 a.m.
(that was me kek)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:50 a.m.
I forget who submitted what requests.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:52 a.m.
Does Default state have any impact with say a Canadian request?
1:52 a.m.
Like BC for British Columbia, AB for Alberta, etc?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:55 a.m.
Yes, because it will be used when doing reverse geolocation.
1:55 a.m.
So unless someone submits Vancouver, BC the geolocate will add Vancouver, WA, USA
1:56 a.m.
If someone says Red Deer without a province or country, it will append AB, CA if that's what is in the config. (edited)
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:57 a.m.
Maybe I should bring this up too, because when we mesh with the west coast (hopefully eventually) aren't local.<channel> going to be saturated? Maybe it should be encouraged to use a prefix other than local. like the airport or something
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:57 a.m.
It helps solve for situations where there's like 24 different Paris's in the US.
1:58 a.m.
We've started using #bot-<location>
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:58 a.m.
Good call. I should do that too
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:58 a.m.
I've kind of camped on #bot since I have the ur meshbot.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:58 a.m.
#Bot-YQL
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:58 a.m.
Yup. Our general practice is only one bot per channel.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 1:58 a.m.
I'll worry about that when we mesh lol
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 1:58 a.m.
That lets a user pick where they want to get their reply from.
1:59 a.m.
Yeah, I realized there's a bit of a trap coded into the bot that I didn't anticipate.
1:59 a.m.
If someone starts with a ping a bot will respond pong, but then the bot will hear pong and respond ping and back and forth.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2025-12-29 2:00 a.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 2:00 a.m.

2:00 a.m.
Luckily the mitigation is removing a single line.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-29 12:40 p.m.
I just made a pretty crucial update to the open_meteo plugin. I enabled it for a while and had a user get weather for Olympia Greece instead of Olympia Washington because it wasn't using the default state and default country variables when doing geolocation.
12:40 p.m.
It's in dev now.

Deep-Thought 2025-12-31 7:48 a.m.
How does the TCP connection work? This message in the readme confuses me "Ensure your MeshCore device has TCP/IP connectivity (e.g., via gateway or bridge)" I thought that's just compantion protocol via TCP. But that sounds more like the tcp repeater method. But they would be fundamentally different.
Deep-Thought
How does the TCP connection work? This message in the readme confuses me "Ensure your MeshCore device has TCP/IP connectivity (e.g., via gateway or bridge)" I thought that's just compantion protocol via TCP. But that sounds more like the tcp repeater method. But they would be fundamentally different.
Nemo 🇨🇭 2026-01-03 5:45 a.m.
I've read somewhere that it's possible to connect ESP32-based MeshCore Companions via WiFi to a local network. Perhaps this is one possibility for "Companion Protocol via TCP"? (I'm using nrf52-based devices, a Companion via BLE, a Reperater via USB)
mini_ash 2026-01-03 6:31 a.m.
is they a way to lock keywords to set channels
LittleTiTi
Hi , Great job on your excellent bot! Would it be possible to integrate a weather alert option? https://github.com/recrof/MeshCore-WeatherBot/ (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-04 1:13 a.m.
Hi, thanks for your ideas from a month ago. I had some time off for the holidays, so I made a lot of progress. I've implemented this as a service plugin for the bot. I've been running it for several weeks now, and it announces the weather like a champ. I haven't had any local lightning storms, but I tested the functionality with some lightning storms elsewhere in the world and it worked great. This feature will be in the next release.
LittleTiTi
Hi, I'm bothering you again for a little while... Would it be possible to integrate a Discord/Telegram gateway to send emergency channels or information to these social networks? Thanks again in advance for the work you do... https://github.com/Hude06/MeshCoreDiscordBridge
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-04 1:14 a.m.
I've implemented a MeshCore -> Discord bridge, but I'm hesitant to give external tools the ability to push data through the bot. The next release does have an Announce feature that will make the bot send out an announcement periodically though. It is triggered via the mesh, so it would work in an internet-down situation. That may serve the same goal.
1
mini_ash
is they a way to lock keywords to set channels
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-04 1:23 a.m.
Yes, commands have a setting that is part of the base command class. The default behavior is to inherit the global permissions list, but if channels = <list> is set for any command it overrides that list. The alternative behaviors are noted in the config.ini.example. Not Set (Commented or Absent): Command uses global monitor_channels from [Bot] section [Joke_Command] # channels = general,#bot,#jokes # Commented = uses global monitor_channels Empty Value: Command becomes DM-only (disabled for all channels) [Joke_Command] channels = # Empty = DM-only mode Channel List: Command restricted to specific channels [Joke_Command] channels = general,#bot,#jokes # Only works in these channels
1
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Hi, thanks for your ideas from a month ago. I had some time off for the holidays, so I made a lot of progress. I've implemented this as a service plugin for the bot. I've been running it for several weeks now, and it announces the weather like a champ. I haven't had any local lightning storms, but I tested the functionality with some lightning storms elsewhere in the world and it worked great. This feature will be in the next release.

@[PNW] HerculesMulligan did you test those improved nixos tests? You can quite easily by installing nix (no need for nixos) and running nix flake check in the repo.

12:36 p.m.

AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-08 9:39 p.m.

AEMON_GREMM
ok not so dumb question, how do i set the heltec frequency? minicom doesnt respond. the consolf on the flasher doesnt respond
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-08 9:42 p.m.
Ah, the companion doesn’t use a terminal. You could set it with MeshCore-CLI, or on a computer with web serial, Connect it with https://app.meshcore.nz/. Click connect, cancel the dialog, and click the three dot menu to connect via USB. I need to add a config script to make that easier.
MeshCore
The MeshCore Companion App

ah thanks. i didnt know of that site.. thanks
AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-08 10:06 p.m.
its working thanks

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-08 11:19 p.m.
Thoughts on this format for the output for the MeshCore -> Discord bridge? It's using webhooks instead of a Discord bot, because I don't even want to mess with Discord -> Mesh. The icons are configurable per-bot using DiceBear.
AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-09 12:39 a.m.
How does the SNR and RSSI work? last hop i guess?
AEMON_GREMM
How does the SNR and RSSI work? last hop i guess?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-09 12:41 a.m.
Exactly. I don't report that anymore on my bot. I use test = "ack @[{sender}]{phrase_part} | {path} | Path Dist: {path_distance} | F/L Dist: {firstlast_distance} | Received: {timestamp}"

Kepler - YQLMesh
2026-01-09 23:28:52,503 - modern_web_viewer - INFO - Cleaned up 10 old packet stream entries (older than 7 days) 2026-01-10 00:28:52,528 - modern_web_viewer - INFO - Cleaned up 3 old packet stream entries (older than 7 days) 2026-01-10 01:28:52,553 - modern_web_viewer - INFO - Cleaned up 4 old packet stream entries (older than 7 days) 2026-01-10 02:28:52,581 - modern_web_viewer - INFO - Cleaned up 26 old packet stream entries (older than 7 days)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-10 11:02 a.m.
Okay. I’ll take a look at the db cleanup code to see if I can catch any issues.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-10 12:41 p.m.
Okay, I see an issue. I had cleanup happening in both the bot process and the webviewer process. My guess is we ended up with lock contention that took down the webviewer process. I moved some error logging from DEBUG to ERROR, and removed the cleanup from the bot process.

1:32 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-10 7:23 p.m.
Good to hear. I think that was the issue. I'm going to push a fun change to the monitoring page. I have @🌲 Tree 's meshcore-decoder running on the client side, so you can pick apart any packet you see go by and decode any hashtag channel messages if the channel is known to the radio, is set in Channels_List, or is added to a new config setting in Web_Viewer.
2
1
1
7:24 p.m.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-11 2:42 p.m.
Just released meshcore-bot v0.7. This includes a number of usability and stability improvements, better configuration defaults, as well as @Deep-Thought's changes to the Nix packaging and @crisprCAS9's magic8 command and {elapsed} placeholder. It also includes optional service plugins for uploading data to @🌲 Tree's Analyzer, to @recrof's map, a Meshcore -> Discord bridge, and an implementation of recrof's weather alerts that was requested. It also (hopefully) resolves the issues that were causing the web viewer to crash periodically for some users. Additionally, I've included a minimal config.ini for users who only want the testing features enabled. Full release notes here: https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot/releases/tag/v0.7
Release v0.7 · agessaman/meshcore-bot
Release Notes New Features Service Plugin Architecture: Implemented service plugin system for background services that run alongside the bot Packet Capture Service: Captures packets from MeshCore ...
5

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-11 5:46 p.m.

5:46 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-11 11:46 p.m.
Back to working on features on dev. Live stream now shows whether the radio heard repeats on a particular response. Eventually, my thought is to add an option to trigger re-transmission if the bot doesn't hear its message repeated at least once.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-12 12:17 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-12 10:01 p.m.
The reason that your weather command is truncating is partly because of a design decision in channel messages.
10:02 p.m.
Your name "YQLmesh.com Bot" takes 15 characters of the available 150, or 10% of the total message length.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2026-01-12 10:02 p.m.
Ahhhh
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-12 10:03 p.m.
I use HowlBot for my bot's name, which ends up at 11 bytes.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2026-01-12 10:04 p.m.
YQLMesh is also 11
10:04 p.m.
I will change it
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-12 10:05 p.m.
I added dynamic message length calculation, but I think I might have missed subtracting one byte. The format is <name>: <message> I'll have to check if I subtracted one or two bytes for the separator.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2026-01-12 10:06 p.m.
Every B counts
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-12 10:06 p.m.

Kepler - YQLMesh

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-13 12:13 a.m.
Indeed. Just tested the wx and gwx commands and verified that they are now working. Also the gwx command now has much better logic for assessing which city globally should be returned. Also, you can re-run the installer install-service.sh to copy the files to your install directory. It will only update new files.

1:51 a.m.
Maybe I have this done wrong
1:51 a.m.
# Greeting message template (default for all channels) # Available fields: {sender} - the user's name/ID # For multi-part greetings, separate messages with pipe (|) # Example (single): "Welcome to the mesh, @[{sender}]!" # Example (multi-part): "Welcome to the mesh, @[{sender}]!|This is a great place to chat.|Use !help for commands." greeting_message = Welcome to YQLMesh, @[{sender}]! | PM me !help for commands. # Include mesh network information in greeting # true: Add mesh statistics to greeting (total contacts, repeaters, etc.) # false: Only send the greeting message include_mesh_info = true # Mesh info format template # Available fields: {total_contacts}, {repeaters}, {companions}, {recent_activity_24h} # Example: "\n\nMesh Info: {total_contacts} contacts, {repeaters} repeaters" # Note: Mesh info is appended to the last greeting message part mesh_info_format = \\n Mesh Info: {total_contacts} contacts, {repeaters} repeaters, {recent_activity_24h} active in last 24h
heyitsmave 2026-01-13 3:05 a.m.
Hey! I’ve been digging into the repeater status features of the bot. Would it be possible to add an API endpoint that can fetch and expose the battery percentage for multiple repeaters in the mesh? I’m looking to pull that data into a Grafana dashboard so I can monitor my whole site’s power health in one place. Even as a low-priority feature, having a JSON API that lists battery levels for all known/configured repeaters would be a huge addition!
AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-13 9:50 a.m.
and post when a new repeater is found
heyitsmave
Hey! I’ve been digging into the repeater status features of the bot. Would it be possible to add an API endpoint that can fetch and expose the battery percentage for multiple repeaters in the mesh? I’m looking to pull that data into a Grafana dashboard so I can monitor my whole site’s power health in one place. Even as a low-priority feature, having a JSON API that lists battery levels for all known/configured repeaters would be a huge addition!
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-13 12:37 p.m.
I'm not opposed, but it would take some time to get just right. A few months ago saw how meshcore-ha caused massive issues on our mesh when it did this—one node produced 50% of the mesh's traffic requesting repeater statuses. My goal with the bot project has not been to introduce features that when configured or managed badly turn out to be mesh-breaking. I'll put it in the Under Consideration section of my roadmap document.
Kepler - YQLMesh
# Greeting message template (default for all channels) # Available fields: {sender} - the user's name/ID # For multi-part greetings, separate messages with pipe (|) # Example (single): "Welcome to the mesh, @[{sender}]!" # Example (multi-part): "Welcome to the mesh, @[{sender}]!|This is a great place to chat.|Use !help for commands." greeting_message = Welcome to YQLMesh, @[{sender}]! | PM me !help for commands. # Include mesh network information in greeting # true: Add mesh statistics to greeting (total contacts, repeaters, etc.) # false: Only send the greeting message include_mesh_info = true # Mesh info format template # Available fields: {total_contacts}, {repeaters}, {companions}, {recent_activity_24h} # Example: "\n\nMesh Info: {total_contacts} contacts, {repeaters} repeaters" # Note: Mesh info is appended to the last greeting message part mesh_info_format = \\n Mesh Info: {total_contacts} contacts, {repeaters} repeaters, {recent_activity_24h} active in last 24h

A few months ago saw how meshcore-ha caused massive issues on our mesh when it did this—one node produced 50% of the mesh's traffic requesting repeater statuses. My goal with the bot project has not been to introduce features that when configured or managed badly turn out to be mesh-breaking. I'll put it in the Under Consideration section of my roadmap document.

3:06 p.m.
It looks like the original message isn't being appended to, and the 2nd message might be hitting a rate limit and not sending?
3:11 p.m.
Is it possible to customize the weather message? Use instead of the arrow emoji for the wind, remove dew point, view distance, etc? Might be good to remove stuff I don't think people will care about
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-13 3:37 p.m.
The arrow emoji is showing wind direction. I'm open to feedback on it.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-13 3:48 p.m.
Ah, I implemented the greeter as a command and that meant that subsequent message responses were getting blocked by the user rate limiter. I added an option to ignore the user rate limiter for system triggered responses.
3:48 p.m.
Fixed now.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
The arrow emoji is showing wind direction. I'm open to feedback on it.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-13 9:01 p.m.
Do you have weather_provider = noaa set or weather_provider = openmeteo? Do other commands that require internet work? solar or gwx or satpass? If the bot doesn't have internet, that won't work. It tries to query 8.8.8.8:53 for DNS, then tries a query to http://1.1.1.1 via HTTP. If those both fail, it tries to fetch http://www.google.com -- if those are blocked at a firewall or something, the bot will think it is offline.
AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-13 9:03 p.m.
weather_provider = noaa
9:03 p.m.
yes has internet
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-13 9:04 p.m.
Are you in the US? If not, probably should switch to openmeteo.

9:06 p.m.

9:06 p.m.

4:37 a.m.

VE6CV
ok here's a few files to look at and see which fits best https://westlethbridgeweather.com/summary.txt https://westlethbridgeweather.com/latest.txt https://westlethbridgeweather.com/plaintext.txt and I will look into the JSON thing
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-18 8:44 p.m.
I have the parser working for the latest.txt file. It parses it into 3 hour buckets, and then condenses it into "Lethbridge: Mostly Cloudy 48.7°F 73%RH 3% PoP | H:76.3°F L:48.7°F | Tomorrow: 60.8°F/48.6°F" (these are pulled from the May values that are in the file right now) Users can also request a daily forecast that focuses on high/low and most common conditions, or an hourly forecast that focuses on temps and precip. I'll go through with emojifying it for readability once I have all the bugs worked out.
VE6CV 2026-01-18 9:04 p.m.
oh that sounds cool!!! Love to see it in action
9:05 p.m.
now... how can we parse that into degrees C instead of F
VE6CV
now... how can we parse that into degrees C instead of F
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-18 9:24 p.m.
It’s using my bot’s config. I handle all temps internally in C, distances in km, etc and then convert for display depending on the bot’s config.
9:26 p.m.
(I’m forcing the Seattle mesh to learn km, because our mesh is international. But C is too much—C is how water feels, F is how people feel. )
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-18 9:35 p.m.
Distances in time, Seattle traffic.
Kepler - YQLMesh 2026-01-19 3:32 p.m.
@[PNW] HerculesMulligan Please stop me if I am being annoying, but I had a great idea for another bot feature. I know there is a dedicated bot for this somewhere that is hardwired I think for the seattle transit, but it pulls information from a GTFS feed to provide the most up to date transit information upon request. It might be a cool module to add to the bot where you can point it to a GTFS feed and have it parse for that information so it's modular and not hardcoded
3:32 p.m.
https://www.transit.land/feeds/f-lethbridgetransit
Lethbridge Transit • GTFS feed details: f-lethbridgetransit
This is a GTFS feed with data for Lethbridge Transit with the Onestop ID of "f-lethbridgetransit". There are 22 versions of this feed.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-19 4:52 p.m.
I think I understand what is going on, but I want to be sure. I'm curious about your config. Do you have the state field set? # Default state for city name disambiguation # When users type "wx seattle", it will search for "seattle, WA, USA" # Use 2-letter state abbreviation (e.g., WA, CA, NY, TX) default_state = WA # Default country for city name disambiguation (for international weather plugin) # Use 2-letter country code (e.g., US, CA, GB, AU) default_country = US
LittleTiTi 2026-01-19 5:05 p.m.
Yes, I gave it a # but the problem persists.
5:07 p.m.

LittleTiTi

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-19 5:09 p.m.

LittleTiTi

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-19 5:09 p.m.

5:09 p.m.
It may be possible to implement now with the Feeds functionality, and I just need to look at the feed structure and how to configure the feeds json.
LittleTiTi 2026-01-19 5:11 p.m.
All my trust is in you
5:13 p.m.
@[PNW] HerculesMulligan I figured it out, you don't need to put a # in front of the line, just leave it as is and leave no value in it... It works....
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-19 5:15 p.m.
That was my suspicion. That will do a lookup on Lyon, , FR by default, and nominatim will just ignore the extra punctuation. There was a fallback that was occuring and with it commented out it was looking up Lyon, WA, FR which was failing. I'm removing the fallback in the next version, so commenting it out will work.
1
5:17 p.m.
These are the kind of decisions one doesn't expect to matter when this thing started out as a personal project for a single bot. (edited)
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-19 8:41 p.m.
Implementing a queue for commands that arrive during the last 5 seconds of a cooldown window has significantly improved the bot's response rate.
1
8:42 p.m.
Instead of ignoring a command that shows up with 0.7 seconds left on the cooldown, it queues it and fires it a second later.
8:42 p.m.
Limit 1 command queued per user, and the per-user timeout is still enforced.
1

7:54 p.m.

AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-21 8:04 p.m.
nope looks like its stored on the device.
8:06 p.m.
not sure if its actaully used however
8:06 p.m.
web page suggests not
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-21 8:12 p.m.
Contacts are stored on the device, because otherwise DMs don't work. If you set auto_manage_contacts = bot the bot will automatically add only companions to your radio, and remove the oldest inactive contacts when it needs more space.
8:13 p.m.
Then you'll get logs like this: 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - ✅ Successfully purged companion jkjk: Auto-purge - contact limit management 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - DEBUG - Companion purge process completed successfully for jkjk 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - 🗑️ Auto-purged companion: jkjk (DM: never, Advert: 2026-01-17T01:48:30.725635, Inactive: 4d) 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - DEBUG - ✅ Verified: contact 'Goose' successfully removed from device 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - ✅ Successfully purged companion Goose: Auto-purge - contact limit management 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - DEBUG - Companion purge process completed successfully for Goose 2026-01-21 17:13:02 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - 🗑️ Auto-purged companion: Goose (DM: never, Advert: 2026-01-17T06:35:29.840719, Inactive: 4d)
AEMON_GREMM 2026-01-21 8:20 p.m.
ok
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-21 8:35 p.m.
It will periodically purge your contact count down to 80% when it hits 95% utilization.
8:36 p.m.
They're all still stored in the contacts table, so you won't lose any permanently. It also won't purge any contacts in your ACL.

# Custom weather sources (extensible pattern, similar to Channels_List) # Format: custom.<provider>.<name> = <source_url> # custom.wxsim.default = <url> - Default source used when 'wx' is called without a location # custom.wxsim.<name> = <url> - Named source accessible via 'wx <name>' # # Example: # custom.wxsim.default = https://westlethbridgeweather.com/latest.txt # custom.wxsim.lethbridge = https://westlethbridgeweather.com/latest.txt # custom.wxsim.seattle = https://seattleweather.example.com/plaintext.txt # # Note: Only one 'default' source can be set per provider type. # Future providers (e.g., custom.pwaweather.location) will follow the same pattern.

heyitsmave 2026-01-22 3:47 p.m.
Hey everyone, I'm seeing a weird error in my logs after enabling verbose=true on the map uploader. Is this a known bug or a configuration issue on my end? 2026-01-22 22:43:35 - MeshCoreBot - ERROR - Failed to load service map_uploader_service: Not a boolean: true # Enable map uploader 2026-01-22 22:43:35 - MeshCoreBot - DEBUG - Traceback (most recent call last): File "/opt/meshcore-bot/modules/service_plugin_loader.py", line 91, in load_service enabled = self.bot.config.getboolean(config_section, 'enabled', fallback=False) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/lib/python3.11/configparser.py", line 844, in getboolean return self._get_conv(section, option, self._convert_to_boolean, ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/lib/python3.11/configparser.py", line 824, in _get_conv return self._get(section, conv, option, raw=raw, vars=vars, ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/lib/python3.11/configparser.py", line 819, in _get return conv(self.get(section, option, **kwargs)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/lib/python3.11/configparser.py", line 1182, in _convert_to_boolean raise ValueError('Not a boolean: %s' % value) ValueError: Not a boolean: true # Enable map uploader
3:54 p.m.
Oh yes, it's a bug. If you leave "[MapUploader]enabled = true # Enable map uploader" with the comment line, it throws that error. I removed the comment line and it works as intended. (edited)

LittleTiTi
Hi, Is it possible to secure the webpage? To leave it entirely viewable. And if modifications are made, will they be password-protected or otherwise
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-28 9:57 a.m.
nginx reverse proxy is a great option, caddy would also work. I already had tailscale set up, so I just access it off the host machine using that. I'd strongly advise against exposing it directly to the open internet directly. I don't know that I'll ever invest the time to harden it to that extent.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Well, that didn't take many lines of code. The next version of the bot will update the radio's name to the bot name (if it is set in the config).

LittleTiTi
This isn't a reverse proxy issue. I don't want people adding channels, deleting contacts, clearing the cache, etc., and this should be managed by the app.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-28 10:58 a.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Do you want to expose parts of the backend to users?
LittleTiTi 2026-01-28 2:44 p.m.
By default, everything should be visible but view-only. Any action should require a password, whether in channel management, Greeted Users, or actions Contact Activity.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-28 3:17 p.m.
I'm unlikely to implement an authentication system at this point. I can add it to the list of feature requests. If you are deploying this in a setting where you are concerned about other users on the lan accessing it, don't bind to 0.0.0.0. It can be locally accessible or put it behind a nginx proxy with authentication enabled. If you want to implement authentication, I'll always consider PRs.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-31 12:17 p.m.

mosssupply 2026-01-31 1:20 p.m.
High level why GTFS is annoying to work with: it’s all centered around trips rather than stops and routes. As a result you have to do a ton of lookups for every query to dedupe real and scheduled buses.
1:22 p.m.
And you could make that less annoying with proper database schema but I never got around to that

Matiko 2026-02-03 8:40 a.m.
Hi, could you help me? My bot has been running for several days and has 240 contacts, but my web viewer shows that everything except the real-time data is empty. Here is my config.ini:⁨⁨[Web_Viewer] # Enable or disable the web data viewer # SECURITY NOTE: Web viewer has NO AUTHENTICATION built-in # Only enable if you understand the security implications # true: web viewer is available for viewing bot data # false: web viewer is disabled (RECOMMENDED for production) enabled = true # Web viewer host address #SECURITY WARNING: # - 0.0.0.0: Accessible from any network interface (INSECURE - exposes all bot data to network) # # Using 0.0.0.0 will expose: # - All messages and their contents # - Contact list and repeater information # - Real-time packet stream # - Bot configuration details # # WITHOUT ANY AUTHENTICATION OR ENCRYPTION host = 0.0.0.0 # Web viewer port # Must be between 1024-65535 (non-privileged ports) # Default: 8080 port = 8080 # Enable debug mode for the web viewer # true: Enable Flask debug mode (auto-reload on changes) # false: Production mode (recommended) debug = false # Auto-start web viewer with bot # true: Start web viewer automatically when bot starts # false: Start web viewer manually (recommended) auto_start = true # Database path for web viewer data # Default: bot_data.db db_path = /data/meshcore_bot.db # Additional hashtag channels to decode in the packet stream # The web viewer can decrypt GroupText messages from hashtag channels # without adding them to the radio. Enter channel names (with or without #) # as a comma-separated list. The Public channel is always included. # # Example: #CQ,#ARES,#EmComm # This will allow decryption of messages on #CQ, #ARES, and #EmComm channels # in addition to any channels configured on your radio. # # Note: Only hashtag channels work here - custom channels with private keys # must be added to the radio itself. decode_hashtag_channels =⁩⁩ (edited)

For BOT-Discord communication to work properly, does the Discord application need to be installed on Raspberry?
Matiko
Hi, could you help me? My bot has been running for several days and has 240 contacts, but my web viewer shows that everything except the real-time data is empty. Here is my config.ini:⁨⁨[Web_Viewer] # Enable or disable the web data viewer # SECURITY NOTE: Web viewer has NO AUTHENTICATION built-in # Only enable if you understand the security implications # true: web viewer is available for viewing bot data # false: web viewer is disabled (RECOMMENDED for production) enabled = true # Web viewer host address #SECURITY WARNING: # - 0.0.0.0: Accessible from any network interface (INSECURE - exposes all bot data to network) # # Using 0.0.0.0 will expose: # - All messages and their contents # - Contact list and repeater information # - Real-time packet stream # - Bot configuration details # # WITHOUT ANY AUTHENTICATION OR ENCRYPTION host = 0.0.0.0 # Web viewer port # Must be between 1024-65535 (non-privileged ports) # Default: 8080 port = 8080 # Enable debug mode for the web viewer # true: Enable Flask debug mode (auto-reload on changes) # false: Production mode (recommended) debug = false # Auto-start web viewer with bot # true: Start web viewer automatically when bot starts # false: Start web viewer manually (recommended) auto_start = true # Database path for web viewer data # Default: bot_data.db db_path = /data/meshcore_bot.db # Additional hashtag channels to decode in the packet stream # The web viewer can decrypt GroupText messages from hashtag channels # without adding them to the radio. Enter channel names (with or without #) # as a comma-separated list. The Public channel is always included. # # Example: #CQ,#ARES,#EmComm # This will allow decryption of messages on #CQ, #ARES, and #EmComm channels # in addition to any channels configured on your radio. # # Note: Only hashtag channels work here - custom channels with private keys # must be added to the radio itself. decode_hashtag_channels =⁩⁩ (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-03 9:39 a.m.
It looks like your db_path is likely different in the [Bot] config section and your [Web_Viewer] config section. They should have the same path, and then they'll work. (I'm adding that to my list of things to clean up.)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
It looks like your db_path is likely different in the [Bot] config section and your [Web_Viewer] config section. They should have the same path, and then they'll work. (I'm adding that to my list of things to clean up.)
Matiko 2026-02-03 9:40 a.m.
I have same path in both places
9:42 a.m.
⁨[Bot] ... # Database path for main bot database # Default: meshcore_bot.db db_path = /data/meshcore_bot.db⁩
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-03 10:03 a.m.
I'm scratching my head a bit. The webviewer is clearly not accessing loading the database, but you're giving it an absolute path. The reason that the live feed works is that data is sent directly to the webviewer. If you set this: ⁨[Logging] log_level = DEBUG⁩ And then load the web viewer, do you see any errors?
Matiko 2026-02-03 10:05 a.m.
I⁨m not sure about how this work in python, but you have hardcoded here db_path and I⁩m not seeing overriding it based on config.ini
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-03 10:06 a.m.
That's setting a fallback, so if we don't specify a value it goes with a database in the same directory as the script root.
Matiko 2026-02-03 10:08 a.m.
It work for my db path when i change this by hand
10:08 a.m.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-03 10:09 a.m.
Hmm... very interesting. I'll need to check where I'm not resolving the path correctly. That'll be fixed shortly.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Hmm... very interesting. I'll need to check where I'm not resolving the path correctly. That'll be fixed shortly.

Raptowny 2026-02-04 6:37 a.m.
Hi, I have a question about DiscordBridge. Will the bot only transfer messages it receives to Discord? I see that users write to the bot (on Discord) but I don't see the answers the bot gives. (edited)
Raptowny
Hi, I have a question about DiscordBridge. Will the bot only transfer messages it receives to Discord? I see that users write to the bot (on Discord) but I don't see the answers the bot gives. (edited)
mini_ash 2026-02-04 8:13 a.m.
it is only what the node sees in the real world and past over to discord the bot cant read discord messages or transmit them
1
Raptowny 2026-02-04 8:14 a.m.
What I mean is that you can see the messages that users write, but you can't see the messages that the BOT sends.
Raptowny
What I mean is that you can see the messages that users write, but you can't see the messages that the BOT sends.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-04 8:52 a.m.
Yes, currently the bridge only processes the RX queue. In my area, people use it to push the Public channel to discord, so users can keep up with the chat even when out of coverage. I can look at options to include the bot's TX in the queue as well. It'll go on my list.
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Yes, currently the bridge only processes the RX queue. In my area, people use it to push the Public channel to discord, so users can keep up with the chat even when out of coverage. I can look at options to include the bot's TX in the queue as well. It'll go on my list.
Raptowny 2026-02-04 8:56 a.m.
thx for reply

5:19 p.m.

5:21 p.m.
So, I installed this, looking for a way to monitor our mesh...specifically our repeaters. I'd like to be able to see their SNR, RSSI, ping times to zero-hop neighbors, current neighbors, etc. Can I do any of that with this project?
5:23 p.m.
Basically lookign for a way to visualize our mesh, and make sure that our infratstructure is staying up and healthy.
ThatGuy
So, I installed this, looking for a way to monitor our mesh...specifically our repeaters. I'd like to be able to see their SNR, RSSI, ping times to zero-hop neighbors, current neighbors, etc. Can I do any of that with this project?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-04 5:24 p.m.
The mesh graph infers the zero-hop neighbors, but because of prefix conflicts that is a guess at best. There's no way to see SNR/RSSI between non-neighbor repeaters... but I have been thinking about writing a module that would do some automated graph walking using traces in the early morning hours.
5:27 p.m.
The contacts page has some features that will help with monitoring infrastructure—you can see recent adverts and the paths that messages take to get to a repeater. I probably should add some more purely monitoring features. We could definitely identify "Probably Offline" and "Adverts Frequently". What would be useful? I'm open to suggestions.
ThatGuy 2026-02-04 5:30 p.m.
Awesome. Let me think on what else would be helpful.
5:32 p.m.
Perhaps being able to enter credientials to a list of repeaters, and periodically connect and download the repeater info page, and put that into a trackable format, so I could go back and look at tends on SNR, etc.
5:33 p.m.
The contacts page does have a lot of good info now.
5:33 p.m.
and the ability to get and decode the output from a repeater's "neighbors" command and show that as well.
ThatGuy
Perhaps being able to enter credientials to a list of repeaters, and periodically connect and download the repeater info page, and put that into a trackable format, so I could go back and look at tends on SNR, etc.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-04 6:02 p.m.

heyitsmave 2026-02-05 4:08 p.m.
Wouldn’t a 12-hour hardcoded interval be an appropriate time frame for this scenario?
heyitsmave
Wouldn’t a 12-hour hardcoded interval be an appropriate time frame for this scenario?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-05 7:04 p.m.
It might be, but the issue was also appropriately staggering the requests. We had some request storms when a user was requesting data too frequently from about 30 repeaters. The lesson was time of day, request interval, and requests per minute all needed to be algorithmic, not user-defined.
heyitsmave
Wouldn’t a 12-hour hardcoded interval be an appropriate time frame for this scenario?
ThatGuy 2026-02-05 10:07 p.m.
I'd think it would depend on the usage scenario. Right now, I'm working on deploying a new mesh in our area with a few other people. There are basically no other MC users in our area at the moment other than our group. We're doing a lot of testing. For regular monitoring of established meshes, yes, a longer period of time with flow controls is better. For deployment testing, something much more frequent would be very useful. If I have to wait for 12 hours for the next update, that doesn't tell me much. I want to see trends, time of day differences, etc. I want to be able to tweak things and see if changes are effective. Etc.
ThatGuy
I'd think it would depend on the usage scenario. Right now, I'm working on deploying a new mesh in our area with a few other people. There are basically no other MC users in our area at the moment other than our group. We're doing a lot of testing. For regular monitoring of established meshes, yes, a longer period of time with flow controls is better. For deployment testing, something much more frequent would be very useful. If I have to wait for 12 hours for the next update, that doesn't tell me much. I want to see trends, time of day differences, etc. I want to be able to tweak things and see if changes are effective. Etc.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-05 10:15 p.m.
This is why I was thinking about pinning it to airtime utilization, number of hops, etc.
10:15 p.m.
Help me understand your monitoring plan better. The bot might be collecting other data that would also be useful.
ThatGuy 2026-02-05 10:18 p.m.
Sure. What I'm looking for is essentinally the meshcore equivalent of ping and traceroute and a good network status tool. I'd like the ability to log and display trends of comms metrics from repeaters such as RSSI, SNR, Uptime, etc. I want to be able to look at a snapshot of our mesh and see if we have repeaters down, and see the health of their RF environment, and how heavy their load is over time. (edited)
ThatGuy
Sure. What I'm looking for is essentinally the meshcore equivalent of ping and traceroute and a good network status tool. WHen trying to access the bot's panel: Internal Server Error The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.
Kepler - YQLMesh
will using --upgrade on the install script move over the settings file as well as upgrade the bot? If I don't use --upgrade is the bot still being upgraded if I git pull and then run the install script?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-07 10:02 p.m.
After git pull, running the install script without --upgrade still upgrades the bot and deps and keeps your config; use --upgrade when you want the service file and service definition to be updated and reloaded as well

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-08 1:23 p.m.
You may need to go into your [Web_Viewer] section in the config and remove the db_path line. From now on, the Web_Viewer will inherit the db_path from [Bot] by default.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-16 8:20 p.m.
Hi, I just released v0.8 of MeshCore-Bot. Release notes: https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot/releases/tag/v0.8 Path & mesh graph — Graph-based route validation with SNR data; mesh graph view in the web viewer with real-time sync New commands — Aurora/space weather, aircraft tracking, configurable command prefixes Global weather — WXSIM custom sources, better ZIP code geocoding, improved international weather support Docker deployment — Dockerfile, compose, device detection, and setup script Config validation — Validation tooling, standardized command sections, per-user rate limiting Testing & docs — pytest suite, MkDocs site with getting-started and upgrade guides Docs are here: https://agessaman.github.io/meshcore-bot/
Release v0.8 · agessaman/meshcore-bot
Release notes New commands &amp; features Aurora — New command for aurora/space weather (NOAA client). Airplanes — Aircraft tracking command. Reload — Admin command to reload configuration without...
Meshcore Bot Documentation
Documentation for the MeshCore bot and its services

Raptowny 2026-02-17 5:34 a.m.
@[PNW] HerculesMulligan What command should I use to update the bot without losing the configuration?
Raptowny
@[PNW] HerculesMulligan What command should I use to update the bot without losing the configuration?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-17 8:49 a.m.
My method has been to ⁨⁨git pull⁩⁩ to update the repo on the device, and then ⁨⁨./install-service.sh --upgrade⁩⁩ will non-destructively copy the files to the installation directory (default: /opt/meshcore-bot/) If config.ini exists there, it will skip copying the file over. (edited)
2

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-17 11:07 a.m.
Upgrade just does the service restart for you as well.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-23 1:51 a.m.
Great to hear it. What was the issue?
AEMON_GREMM 2026-02-23 1:56 a.m.

AEMON_GREMM 2026-02-23 9:41 a.m.
Is there a way, like a conf.d directory, to add plugins that you can load automatically without it being erased on an upgrade?
AEMON_GREMM
Is there a way, like a conf.d directory, to add plugins that you can load automatically without it being erased on an upgrade?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-23 9:49 a.m.
If you add other plugins, the installer should leave them in place. It uses a non-destructive rsync. You can add command overrides to the modules/commands/alternatives directory. If you modify a command, drop it in that directory and it will override the one distributed with the bot.
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-24 7:30 p.m.
Having 4 meshcore-bots running on the BostonMesh has highlighted the issue of bot responses triggering bot requests. The most noticeable is the “Prefix” command - the reply triggers a round of error messages.
7:31 p.m.
For all responses, it might be worth starting them off with a character and/or emoji that prevents the triggering of other bots.
paul / BostonMesh
Having 4 meshcore-bots running on the BostonMesh has highlighted the issue of bot responses triggering bot requests. The most noticeable is the “Prefix” command - the reply triggers a round of error messages.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-24 7:42 p.m.
Good news, half of that is in the most recent release! But I have to ask, are they running in the public channel and/or sharing a channel? (edited)
7:43 p.m.
You can set a trigger prefix in v0.8. I guess I could add a message prefix option.
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-24 7:45 p.m.
The first two that popped up a few months ago were configured to listen in #test. The owners are unknown and I’m pretty sure that they have not been updated. They were also in spotty locations. I set one up about a month ago that is full featured - and I am in a “reliable” location on the mesh. A more recent one spun up further out West, which has been great to watch - but we run into cascading “prefix” triggers.
7:45 p.m.
The new bot added “momjoke” - which has been a hilarious counterpart to the dad jokes in #joke.
paul / BostonMesh
The new bot added “momjoke” - which has been a hilarious counterpart to the dad jokes in #joke.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-24 7:46 p.m.
That's funny. We were just talking about that a few days ago. (I as assuming that they're "your momma..." jokes?)
paul / BostonMesh
The first two that popped up a few months ago were configured to listen in #test. The owners are unknown and I’m pretty sure that they have not been updated. They were also in spotty locations. I set one up about a month ago that is full featured - and I am in a “reliable” location on the mesh. A more recent one spun up further out West, which has been great to watch - but we run into cascading “prefix” triggers.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-24 7:47 p.m.
The immediate solution to fixing that would be to add the other bots' names to the banned_users list.
1
7:48 p.m.
Find this section and use a comma-separated list of their names (it does match from the beginning of the name, so a substring will work too). [Banned_Users] banned_users =
paul / BostonMesh 2026-02-24 7:48 p.m.
I had also expected the “yo mamma” jokes, but he added actual jokes from a mom’s perspective:
  • If I ever go missing, just follow my kids. They can find me wherever I try to hide!
  • It's not easy being a mom. If it were, Dad would do it!
  • Are my kids perfect? No, but we can blame dad for that one!
paul / BostonMesh
I had also expected the “yo mamma” jokes, but he added actual jokes from a mom’s perspective:
  • If I ever go missing, just follow my kids. They can find me wherever I try to hide!
  • It's not easy being a mom. If it were, Dad would do it!
  • Are my kids perfect? No, but we can blame dad for that one!

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-06 7:57 p.m.
With 1.14 out, there's probably interest in supporting multi-byte paths. All of that work is done in the dev branch in the bot's repo, but I've only been able to test it for a few days. We just ran some basic tests on our mesh and it seems to be behaving well enough to deploy if you are looking to live on the bleeding edge.

Skye 2026-03-09 1:49 a.m.
Does the Packet Capture service send the raw packet data to the broker or is it parsed? Does it also require an update for multibyte paths?
Skye 2026-03-09 2:53 a.m.
Just a note on the github readme.md Hardware Setup section could use a little more detail - it just says "Flash MeshCore firmware to your device" I had to do some digging to find out if it was supposed to be companion or repeater. Companion USB or Companion Bluetooth correct? Don't have to mess with building MESH_PACKET_LOGGING, that's just for repeaters?
Skye 2026-03-09 3:01 a.m.
Ah from the Letsmesh setup page for companions: " Unlike repeater or room server nodes, companion observers don't require special firmware. Your MeshCore companion device can log packets with the standard build flags. " that would be helpful in the README
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-12 12:10 a.m.
I just release v0.8.3. This release adds support for parsing multi-byte paths across the bot.
  • Adds a trace command (and its companion tracer, trace reciprocal) that enables the bot to perform traces.
  • Adds a new RandomLine matcher for file-based command responses. Thanks Ian!
  • Adds data-retention rules for all databases and purges old records periodically.
  • Added Earthquake notification service. Thanks davidkjackson54!
  • Adds a Bot → Telegram bridge that enables messages from one or more channels to be posted to Telegram
  • Adds a profanity and hate symbol filter to prevent bad actors on the mesh from getting your Discord banned.
  • Adds support for local service plugins in a separate directory for easy backups. Thanks for the suggestion and testing davidkjackson54!
https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I just release v0.8.3. This release adds support for parsing multi-byte paths across the bot.
  • Adds a trace command (and its companion tracer, trace reciprocal) that enables the bot to perform traces.
  • Adds a new RandomLine matcher for file-based command responses. Thanks Ian!
  • Adds data-retention rules for all databases and purges old records periodically.
  • Added Earthquake notification service. Thanks davidkjackson54!
  • Adds a Bot → Telegram bridge that enables messages from one or more channels to be posted to Telegram
  • Adds a profanity and hate symbol filter to prevent bad actors on the mesh from getting your Discord banned.
  • Adds support for local service plugins in a separate directory for easy backups. Thanks for the suggestion and testing davidkjackson54!
https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-17 1:12 p.m.
I have it on my agenda to switch the message scheduling to a cron format. [Scheduled_Messages] currently only supports messages scheduled at the same time every day, but I'd like to improve that. I'll bump it up my list.

ZeroPoke 2026-03-17 1:35 p.m.
Greetings My meshcore-bot has seems stop working. Im using it with pyMC virtual companion thingy. RasPi 4b 4gig. I was working on plugin and I started to get sime timeout error (at the bottom.) So I turned off my plugin and Im getting these NoneType error and the bot wont reply. 2026-03-17 10:28:29 - MeshCoreBot - ERROR - Connection failed: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'is_connected' 2026-03-17 10:28:29 - MeshCoreBot - ERROR - Failed to connect to MeshCore node NoneType ^^ Timeout vv 2026-03-16 23:32:41 - MeshCoreBot - ERROR - Error processing channel operations: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/opt/meshcore-bot/modules/scheduler.py", line 397, in run_scheduler future.result(timeout=30) # 30 second timeout ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/lib/python3.13/concurrent/futures/_base.py", line 458, in result raise TimeoutError() TimeoutError 2026-03-16 23:33:11 - MeshCoreBot - ERROR - Error processing message queue: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/opt/meshcore-bot/modules/scheduler.py", line 423, in run_scheduler future.result(timeout=30) # 30 second timeout ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/lib/python3.13/concurrent/futures/_base.py", line 458, in result raise TimeoutError() TimeoutError
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-17 1:51 p.m.
That looks like an issue connecting to the radio. Sometimes a reboot of the bot is enough, but sometimes a restart of the actual radio is necessary.
ZeroPoke 2026-03-17 2:36 p.m.
huh this seems to be a bigger issue. Rebooted the device error didnt happen. Bot didnt work. Reinstall the bot. still not working. I guess my pyMC is broke some how.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-17 4:23 p.m.
The “NoneType … is_connected” error means the bot never successfully created a MeshCore connection object; it’s a connection/config/environment problem, not a logic bug in the bot. Do you see a connection occur in the logs on pyMC_repeater?
kasari 2026-03-17 9:17 p.m.
I finally got mine setup, Man what a pain the ars.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-17 9:43 p.m.
Hey thanks. I've had a hard day. Thanks for swinging by to make your contribution.

ZeroPoke 2026-03-18 2:47 a.m.
Oh thanks for the follow up reply I totally missed it till now, Im not sure, Im not 100% sure what I should be looking for in the logs. What I do know is after I sent that last msg. I renamed the companion in pyMC_repeater. And it started working again for awhile. Then after some time the bot would not reponsing, but restarting the service would fix it. Then it would get to the point that rebooting the Pi doesnt help either. This has happened a couple times now. I dont see any related errors while watching meshcore-bot log, msgs just kind stop showing up. Just an error about pruning, that seems to spam while working or not. I havent seen watching the pyMC log when this has happened tho. Just like the companion part stop working correctly and then starts working correctly again sometime later. And I havent had much luck finding any errors.
ZeroPoke
Oh thanks for the follow up reply I totally missed it till now, Im not sure, Im not 100% sure what I should be looking for in the logs. What I do know is after I sent that last msg. Then it would get to the point that rebooting the Pi doesnt help either. This has happened a couple times now. I dont see any related errors while watching meshcore-bot log, msgs just kind stop showing up. Just an error about pruning, that seems to spam while working or not. ZeroPoke 2026-03-18 12:08 p.m.
Thanks for any work you put into this. Your work with meshcore-bot and the companion stuff has been amazing. If there anything I can do to help just let me know. I was thinking after I sent that msg yesterday. That could be related to my config. One thing I had to do to make the tcp timeout to 0 to make the lost more then like a minute or two.
ZeroPoke
Thanks for any work you put into this. Your work with meshcore-bot and the companion stuff has been amazing. If there anything I can do to help just let me know. I was thinking after I sent that msg yesterday. That could be related to my config. One thing I had to do to make the tcp timeout to 0 to make the lost more then like a minute or two.

3:55 p.m.

3:56 p.m.
I'm thinking that we're running into a pyMC issue, or a pyMC + meshcore_py issue.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Just as a test, do you have a firmware companion to connect the bot to? Does it boot?
ZeroPoke 2026-03-18 4:13 p.m.
I do have a Heltec V4 device with companion firmware. And you want me to use the Bluetooth connection for the bot? I did notice that when the bot isn't responding to msgs that pyMC console's new chat feature won't connect.
ZeroPoke
I do have a Heltec V4 device with companion firmware. And you want me to use the Bluetooth connection for the bot? I did notice that when the bot isn't responding to msgs that pyMC console's new chat feature won't connect.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-18 4:18 p.m.
Oh, that's the issue right there. I didn't track that you were using Console. I'm pretty sure that @treehouse ⚡ hasn't implemented the ability to disable the console from connecting to the frame server. When a new connection comes in on the frame server, it evicts the old connection. Basically, the bot is getting into an eviction war with the pyMC_console until it takes down the frame server.
4:19 p.m.
I need to pull the newest version of console and see if that toggle is there now.
treehouse ⚡ 2026-03-18 4:22 p.m.
would a new companion on a new port or IP solve that issue? @[PNW] HerculesMulligan
4:22 p.m.
haven't caught up with this chat, so not fully versed on what the root issue is — but whatever you think the solution is, I can help whip up a fix
treehouse ⚡
haven't caught up with this chat, so not fully versed on what the root issue is — but whatever you think the solution is, I can help whip up a fix
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-18 4:25 p.m.
We just need the ability to specify that the pyMC_console companion client should not connect to certain companion frame servers. (edited)
4:26 p.m.
i.e. "I'm hooking up a bot or another app here, leave this companion alone."
4:28 p.m.
For durability, we could add another parameter to the config.yaml (web_client = false?) under those companions... or you can store prefs.
treehouse ⚡ 2026-03-18 4:29 p.m.
ah. does the toggle switch not solve this issue?
4:29 p.m.
4:29 p.m.
in the config. it should disconnect the companion from the server... but this would be the time to test it, hah

treehouse ⚡ 2026-03-18 4:47 p.m.

treehouse ⚡ 2026-03-18 6:58 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan

Gray Errhoss 2026-03-18 7:00 p.m.

ZeroPoke
or maybe Im jsut full of shit. I just tried a couple ways that it might restart the toggle and didnt reset so maybe I didnt set it.
ZeroPoke 2026-03-18 7:01 p.m.
Well Ive reset it as off. Signed out and rebooted the Pi confirmed its still off. and I guess we will try this again. (edited)
treehouse ⚡
if you make it in repeater, Console's frontend uses it and can control it
ZeroPoke 2026-03-18 7:05 p.m.
Its not about making it. Its about accessing it. So Console has that Chat that lets you chat via whatever Companion. Which may or may not stop the Bot from accessing said companion. So then if I was running Rightups then in theory there is no Chat to lock up access from.
treehouse ⚡ 2026-03-18 7:16 p.m.
console is accessing it like any other companion would. I'm not 100% sure if the frame server can support multiple clients (@[PNW] HerculesMulligan ?) but if it can, it shouldn't be a conflict. it's using the same entry point as any other client would
7:16 p.m.
if the frame server can support multiple clients, then it shouldn't be an issue. but if it can't, or its configured to only accept 1 connection, then you'll have issues
treehouse ⚡
console is accessing it like any other companion would. I'm not 100% sure if the frame server can support multiple clients (@[PNW] HerculesMulligan ?) but if it can, it shouldn't be a conflict. it's using the same entry point as any other client would
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-18 7:20 p.m.
It only supports one client at a time. The binary frame server drains the persisted message data, and any behavior other than evict-on-connect would either need a client id scheme that doesn’t exist in the firmware or would randomly deliver messages to one or the other connected client.
1
7:21 p.m.
I did all my testing with Liam’s app. I need to hook up MeshCore-CLI and some of the other apps to see if there are any inconsistencies that might need to be handled.
7:22 p.m.
It’s possible there’s something about command ordering or response timing that might be throwing off other apps.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Hey thanks. I've had a hard day. Thanks for swinging by to make your contribution.
awhite 2026-03-18 10:03 p.m.
my experience was quite the opposite- installation was smooth and clean and the config options are well documented. Thank you for your work!
1
1
awhite
my experience was quite the opposite- installation was smooth and clean and the config options are well documented. Thank you for your work!
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-18 10:04 p.m.

@[PNW] HerculesMulligan does packet capture service need to be running to report all packets to mqtt? like mqtt is under that service area in the config. so if i enable mqtt connection do i enable the packet capture service to do the work? or does it do it without that enabled?

4:49 p.m.
monitor_channels = bot in [Channels]
4:49 p.m.
channel_keywords commented out, says that should enable all triggers
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-20 5:22 p.m.
Is your channel named #bot or bot on the radio?
5:23 p.m.
It might look like it is commented out, but the config parser will handle hashtags inline for channel values.
5:23 p.m.
Also, is the channel configured on the radio itself? If not, it can be added through the Radio panel in the web viewer.
Skye 2026-03-20 5:32 p.m.
config.ini.example used the format monitor_channels = general,test,emergency so I assumed the leading hashtag wasn't necessary?
5:38 p.m.

5:39 p.m.
2026-03-20 14:36:39 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - Successfully fetched 3 channels from MeshCore node 2026-03-20 14:36:39 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - Channel 0: Public 2026-03-20 14:36:39 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - Channel 1: #bot 2026-03-20 14:36:39 - MeshCoreBot - INFO - Channel 2: #test
5:42 p.m.

5:46 p.m.
I guess the hashtag is required, now it works in #test DEBUG - Channel #bot not in monitored channels: ['bot', '#test']

config.ini.example used the format monitor_channels = general,test,emergency so I assumed the leading hashtag wasn't necessary?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-20 6:57 p.m.

Skye 2026-03-20 9:28 p.m.

Skye 2026-03-21 1:37 p.m.
Would it be possible to have wx_command.py and wx_international.py fall back to bot_latitude/bot_longitude if companion_location is false
1:38 p.m.
Just so people with no location in advert can just use bare command wx or gwx
1:42 p.m.
Also it looks like usage specifies 7d but execute() uses 7day
1:44 p.m.
and wx_international.py doesn't support hourly
1:44 p.m.
would you like me to make a PR?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 1:46 p.m.
I've intentionally had those commands return usage instead of the bot's location. My thinking was that there was a relatively low chance that the user wanted weather for the bot's location, but guidance on how to get the wx for their desired location would be more useful.
1:46 p.m.
I can certainly look at adding an optional config to return the bot's weather instead of usage though.
1:49 p.m.
I'll update the parser to accept 7d in addition to 7day and 7-day. (edited)
Skye 2026-03-21 1:50 p.m.
Thanks, no rush not trying to dump a bunch of work on you just noting things as I set up as a new user
1:51 p.m.
Our mesh is just our city right now so a default value for wx makes sense
1:52 p.m.
and lots of people here don't have location in their companion advert
1:53 p.m.
everyone here is excited to have a bot to use, nice work
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 1:57 p.m.
Sounds good. I'm running a bot on a mesh that spans like 300 km, so the bot's location isn't super useful to a lot of people.
Skye 2026-03-21 1:58 p.m.
oh and poll_weather_alerts_interval = 0 should disable polling? I'm outside the US, it's not noted in config.ini but in the code it says it won't work outside US
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 1:59 p.m.
If you want to test, I've committed changes on dev to modify the no-parameter behavior on both commands if you have [Weather] use_bot_location_when_no_location = true
Skye
oh and poll_weather_alerts_interval = 0 should disable polling? I'm outside the US, it's not noted in config.ini but in the code it says it won't work outside US
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 1:59 p.m.
Yes, true. I need to look for a global source of those weather alerts. It is on my list when there is time.
Skye 2026-03-21 2:00 p.m.
I'll probably write a new wx_canada.py to use Environment Canada when I have time
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 2:01 p.m.
Oh, that would be helpful. I've been meaning to improve the weather command source switching. That would be my push to get it done.
Skye 2026-03-21 2:09 p.m.

1
Skye 2026-03-21 2:59 p.m.
I'd recommend changing the two config.ini examples to a working example eg monitor_channels = #bot,#test,#emergency
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 3:48 p.m.
Thanks. Will do.
3:51 p.m.
Clear enough? [Channels] # Channels to monitor (comma-separated) # Bot will only respond to messages on these channels # Use exact channel names as configured on your MeshCore node # Use hashtags for hashtag channels. (i.e. #mybotchannel, #weather, etc.) monitor_channels = #mybotchannel
3:52 p.m.
We really don't want every bot user spinning up their bot in #bot. That would get chaotic.
Skye 2026-03-21 4:16 p.m.
Looks good!
Skye 2026-03-21 5:30 p.m.

8:03 p.m.

Skye
Another feature request In Edit Feed Subscription -> Output Format it would be very nice to have a {shortlink} that is the {link} run through some link shortener API if that's possible (I guess that would require some new [Feed_Manager] options). My feeds all have really long links.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 8:05 p.m.
Yeah, I'll have to look into what link shorteners have an anonymous public API. I'd expect the list to be relatively small. If you have a preferred one that requires an API key, let me know and I'll add it.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 8:09 p.m.
Ah, yeah. I'll hook that up to the new channel add process.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Yeah, I'll have to look into what link shorteners have an anonymous public API. I'd expect the list to be relatively small. If you have a preferred one that requires an API key, let me know and I'll add it.
Skye 2026-03-21 8:14 p.m.
Looks like v.gd doesn't require a key and provides a preview page so it's a little safer https://v.gd/apishorteningreference.php
v.gd - URL shortening API Reference
URL shortening API Reference (developer documentation)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 8:14 p.m.
Okay. I can wire that up.
Skye 2026-03-21 8:14 p.m.
That's awesome thanks
8:15 p.m.
Each RSS item can only generate one MC message right? I have to squeeze it all into one MC message?
8:18 p.m.
It's fine if it's one message, actually better to be frugal with airtime, just wondering
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 8:19 p.m.
That was my initial design. I recognize that there's reasons to generate more than one. I'm happy to reassess, but pagination could be challenging to format.
Skye
I don't think the +New button works either (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 8:20 p.m.
Works in dev now.
1
Skye 2026-03-21 9:14 p.m.
Might be nice when adding a new feed to have an option "Mark all items as seen" so that the feed starts out only publishing new items instead of spamming the channel with old news until it catches up
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 9:14 p.m.
True.
9:14 p.m.
You're the first person to really play with and give feedback on this part.
Skye 2026-03-21 9:17 p.m.
I'm contacting a lot of EOC managers here (emergency operations centre) to get them to publish RSS feeds of their alerts so hopefully MeshCore can be another tool in the box during disasters. They seem to be happening every year now...
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 9:25 p.m.
Makes sense.
9:25 p.m.
Just pushed the url shortening (it was already used in Weather, but I moved it to a centralized helper): [External_Data] # URL shortener API base (v.gd / is.gd-compatible create.php). Default: https://v.gd # See https://v.gd/apishorteningreference.php short_url_website = https://v.gd # Optional API key for alternate shortener hosts (unused for public v.gd/is.gd) short_url_website_api_key = (edited)
9:26 p.m.
And you can use {url|shorten} on a per placeholder basis or turn on shortening for all urls in the config.ini (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
And you can use {url|shorten} on a per placeholder basis or turn on shortening for all urls in the config.ini (edited)
Skye 2026-03-21 9:43 p.m.
It's working fantastic
9:43 p.m.
Small thing {link|shorten} doesn't work in Preview Format
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 9:44 p.m.
It was working for me… hmmm.
Skye 2026-03-21 9:44 p.m.
oh wait i didnt restart web_viewer
1
9:45 p.m.
There we go Could Preview Format use the value from config feed_user_agent ? I had to change it because one of my feeds was blocking any user-agent with bot (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 9:51 p.m.
That’s likely. I’ll check in a bit.
Skye 2026-03-21 10:08 p.m.
One more feed request before dinner... my Output Format is currently like this {emoji} CBC: {title|truncate:95} {date} {link|shorten} the short link is great because it's fixed width, but I find I'm having to check every value for truncate to fit the maximum title text in a message. It would be great if there was a modifier like {title|auto} that would automatically calculate the remaining space in the message and auto truncate. Only one allowed... (edited)
10:10 p.m.
or |flex or |max-width or |expand or something like that (edited)
10:15 p.m.
{date} is variable width too so I guess it would have to calculate on a second pass after the rest are rendered
Skye
One more feed request before dinner... my Output Format is currently like this {emoji} CBC: {title|truncate:95} {date} {link|shorten} the short link is great because it's fixed width, but I find I'm having to check every value for truncate to fit the maximum title text in a message. It would be great if there was a modifier like {title|auto} that would automatically calculate the remaining space in the message and auto truncate. Only one allowed... (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-21 11:11 p.m.
Okay, {title|auto} exists.
1
Skye 2026-03-21 11:32 p.m.
Perfect. thank you so much
11:33 p.m.
So tidy {emoji} CBC: {title|auto} {link|shorten}
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I'm thinking that we're running into a pyMC issue, or a pyMC + meshcore_py issue.
ZeroPoke 2026-03-22 2:36 a.m.
So my setup has fallen back into the state where the bot stop recieving/responding to messages really quickly. Like sub 5mins. Are there any specific logs I should grab that could help?
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Yes. There’s a toggle in the settings.
James 2026-03-22 5:24 a.m.
could you give me more info on how to edit the settings for meteo api please?
James
could you give me more info on how to edit the settings for meteo api please?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-22 11:47 a.m.
Which settings? OpenMeteo doesn't require an API key, so under the [Weather] section just change: weather_provider = openmeteo and then adjust the other settings to your local preference for units.

James 2026-03-23 6:33 a.m.
how do I stop Nasa from overiding meteo ?
James 2026-03-23 6:50 a.m.
which file in meshcore-bot do I need to edit, to stop Nasa weather overiding #wx ? - going nuts here
James
which file in meshcore-bot do I need to edit, to stop Nasa weather overiding #wx ? - going nuts here
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-23 8:54 a.m.
[Weather] # Weather provider selection # Options: noaa, openmeteo # noaa: Use NOAA API (US-focused, better for US locations, includes weather alerts) # openmeteo: Use Open-Meteo API (global coverage, works worldwide) # Default: noaa weather_provider = openmeteo
8:54 a.m.
You can edit this in your config.ini
1

12:59 a.m.

1:00 a.m.

It understands all of the incoming data, but I haven't validated TX with multibyte paths.

Is there an easy way to route keywords to various channels, instead of all the channels? Also If I delete remove Public from my Companion radio, can I add it back when my bot isn't using it? Yes I'm a n OLD Newbie (77) Jay
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-24 1:50 p.m.
Under any command you can add channels = <channel list> and that command will only work in those channels.

Added today's high/low to weather_alarm --- a/modules/service_plugins/weather_service.py +++ b/modules/service_plugins/weather_service.py @@ -472,6 +472,11 @@ class WeatherService(BaseServicePlugin): wind_dir_str = f"{wind_direction}" if wind_direction else "" forecast_text += f" {wind_dir_str}{wind_speed}{self.wind_speed_unit}" + today_high = int(daily['temperature_2m_max'][0]) + today_low = int(daily['temperature_2m_min'][0]) + # Show high/low with labels to make it clear + forecast_text += f" | H:{today_high}{temp_symbol} L:{today_low}{temp_symbol}" + # Add tomorrow's forecast

Lïttlëaton 2026-03-25 7:07 p.m.
is there a command that can list out repeaters with duplicate keys? (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 7:18 p.m.
Full duplicate keys? Or duplicate prefixes?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Full duplicate keys? Or duplicate prefixes?
Lïttlëaton 2026-03-25 7:20 p.m.
oh yes prefix.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 7:20 p.m.
prefix AA
7:20 p.m.
You can also do multibyte prefix AAAA
Lïttlëaton 2026-03-25 7:22 p.m.
i’d like a command that lists out all repeaters that have duplicate prefix. that i could put on a timer. guess that could max out the char count on a message.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 7:22 p.m.
Yeah. I could definitely do something for the web-viewer, but in a congested mesh that could be 256 entries.
Lïttlëaton 2026-03-25 7:23 p.m.
hmm what about list out the prefix that have dupes?
7:23 p.m.
i’ll rethink this and collect my ideas and get back to you
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 7:26 p.m.
Let's say you get absolutely congested, that's 254 prefixes (00 and FF are reserved). 254 * 3 = 762 characters AA, minus one character for the trailing comma is 761 divided by a typical message size of about 140 characters = 6 messages that are literally just a string of numbers. That's not the end of the world, but it's automated.
Lïttlëaton 2026-03-25 7:26 p.m.
what about when a new repeater is heard in the mesh if it has a dupe prefix the bot auto posts ?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 7:26 p.m.
There's already prefix free that will list prefixes that aren't in use.
Lïttlëaton
what about when a new repeater is heard in the mesh if it has a dupe prefix the bot auto posts ?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 7:28 p.m.
Hmm... that has more utility. We'd subscribe to the new contact event, and if the contact doesn't exist it would post Heard new repeater {name} with prefix {prefix}. {prefixes_free} free prefixes remain. Type 'prefix free' to find one.\
7:29 p.m.
There's also prefix best <location> which attempts to use the mesh's graph to find a repeater for a location that won't have a duplicate neighbor.
2

James
I've sussed out the command - weather longotn uk but still not in metric ? doh
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-25 11:33 p.m.
I think you might have a spelling issue. # Temperature unit for weather display # Options: fahrenheit, celsius # Default: fahrenheit temperature_unit = fahrenheit # Wind speed unit for weather display # Options: mph, kmh, ms (meters per second) # Default: mph wind_speed_unit = mph # Precipitation unit for weather display # Options: inch, mm # Default: inch precipitation_unit = inch

Chris 2026-03-26 6:29 p.m.
fwiw - it looks like in dropping # from the region names, it's now still part of the repeater transport generation or something? tl,dr; I put ioi as a region on my repeater, i need to add it to the config.ini as flood_scope = #ioi otherwise the scope of the packet from the bot doesn't match the scope i set in the repeater
