enabled = false
Skye
enabled = false
Gray Errhoss 2026-03-26 6:43 p.m.
That's just packet capture isnt it?
6:43 p.m.
Or is packet capture required for MQTT
Skye 2026-03-26 6:43 p.m.
Yes
Gray Errhoss 2026-03-26 6:44 p.m.
Ah okay it's not clear as there's a mqtt_enabled too
6:45 p.m.
That with the options right below it really looks like a seperate debugging/packet capture settings
Skye 2026-03-26 6:56 p.m.
It's at the top of the [Section]
Gray Errhoss 2026-03-26 7:06 p.m.
I get it, you think i'm stupid for not changing that (i've been in IT for 12 years I've been on both sides of this), but would still say it's not obvious that you need to enable packet capture, when there is a seperate enable = true for MQTT
7:07 p.m.
To me packet capture = troubleshooting
Skye 2026-03-26 7:12 p.m.
Ok calm down
7:12 p.m.
I'm on a phone sorry if my answers weren't long enough
Gray Errhoss
I get it, you think i'm stupid for not changing that (i've been in IT for 12 years I've been on both sides of this), but would still say it's not obvious that you need to enable packet capture, when there is a seperate enable = true for MQTT
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-26 7:34 p.m.
Yeah... that's my bad. I adapted the packet_capture script from a stand-alone project and mqtt_enabled existed from the time when I was testing the integration but didn't want to pipe bad packets anywhere. I'll delete it from the example config, because it defaults to true. All commands and service plugins use enabled = true|false as the primary activation toggle.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Yeah... that's my bad. I adapted the packet_capture script from a stand-alone project and mqtt_enabled existed from the time when I was testing the integration but didn't want to pipe bad packets anywhere. I'll delete it from the example config, because it defaults to true. All commands and service plugins use enabled = true|false as the primary activation toggle.
Gray Errhoss 2026-03-26 7:40 p.m.
No worries! Thanks!

Chris 2026-03-27 6:23 p.m.
I have a fork that introduces a simple “don’t reply to messages over X hops” for public channels. Also changed the example configs so they start with a region (robot) and multibyte (3), so the defaults get a newbie a locally successful bot but has some caps they’d hopefully engage with their local mesh (if it’s exists) before changing
6:24 p.m.
Threw an AGENTS.md in it also, as a preventative measure so hopefully folks asking openclaw to deploy don’t undo those kinds of throttles unknowingly and then had a few agents try to bypass it
Chris
I have a fork that introduces a simple “don’t reply to messages over X hops” for public channels. Also changed the example configs so they start with a region (robot) and multibyte (3), so the defaults get a newbie a locally successful bot but has some caps they’d hopefully engage with their local mesh (if it’s exists) before changing
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-27 7:28 p.m.
I like the max hops reply filter. I totally see the issue that is designed to solve—both tropoducting and when meshes merge with bots on the same channel. I'm less convinced by the change to the configs, mostly because I'm imagining getting bombarded with requests that are basically, "Why my bot not respond?" that I have to diagnose. In a similar style of change, I have considered adding a yes_i_really_want_to_run_my_bot_on_public_and_i_recognize_that_it_is_probably_really_bad_for_mesh_health_and_also_annoying = true setting before the bot will respond on Public. I haven't played with OpenClaw, but it does probably make sense to include some basic instructions so agents don't screw up user settings. Send over a PR and I'll look at getting it merged. My initial thought with the regions line might be start commented out and get phased in once Liam's regions solution gets a wider rollout and more people are actively using regions.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I like the max hops reply filter. I totally see the issue that is designed to solve—both tropoducting and when meshes merge with bots on the same channel. I'm less convinced by the change to the configs, mostly because I'm imagining getting bombarded with requests that are basically, "Why my bot not respond?" that I have to diagnose. In a similar style of change, I have considered adding a yes_i_really_want_to_run_my_bot_on_public_and_i_recognize_that_it_is_probably_really_bad_for_mesh_health_and_also_annoying = true setting before the bot will respond on Public. I haven't played with OpenClaw, but it does probably make sense to include some basic instructions so agents don't screw up user settings. Send over a PR and I'll look at getting it merged. My initial thought with the regions line might be start commented out and get phased in once Liam's regions solution gets a wider rollout and more people are actively using regions.
Chris 2026-03-27 7:30 p.m.
yeah the config one was more putting stuff in comments. I can polish it up and send it over
Chris
yeah the config one was more putting stuff in comments. I can polish it up and send it over
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-27 7:30 p.m.
Sounds like a plan. Can you target dev?
1
Chris 2026-03-27 7:35 p.m.
openclaw/nanoclaw now makes it so you just have bots reading your repo and finding instructions and deploying it, so even if you don't want to do AI dev, or take AI PRs, someone is gonna ask openclaw to setup a meshcore bot, and it's gonna goggle it and run hog wild. Most of the AGENTS.md is so when the user asks "this doesn't respond to people after 8 hops" claw doesn't go "You're right, it appears theres a bug, i've removed it (eg, removed the function)" instead if says "There is a cap on how many hops the bot will reply to by default because airtime on the mesh is a limited resource shared with other people, when you get a message with 8 or more hops, it might not be intended for your bot, would you like to change that to 10 if you know your mesh is that big?" Or tries to turn it into an RSS news reader thinking an unscoped private channel is like a private channel on discord
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Hmm... that has more utility. We'd subscribe to the new contact event, and if the contact doesn't exist it would post Heard new repeater {name} with prefix {prefix}. {prefixes_free} free prefixes remain. Type 'prefix free' to find one.\

New feature is working. Will let it run a bit more.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Hi Jay. We have a current weather tool that queries a flat-file weather forecast for specific locations that could probably be adapted for your purpose. What is the structure of the payload, as an example?
James 2026-03-29 8:10 p.m.
If you mean this structure it's :- msg.payload = JSON.parse(msg.payload); if (msg.payload.device == ("231")) { var newtemp = msg.payload.temperature_F - 32 ; newtemp = newtemp * 5 ; newtemp = newtemp / 9; newtemp = parseFloat(newtemp).toFixed(2); var hum = msg.payload.humidity; hum = hum - 0; var value ="Date/Time: " + msg.payload.time + "\nTemp: "+ newtemp + " deg.C " + "Humidity: " +hum +"%"; msg.payload = value; return msg;
8:12 p.m.
That gets saved to a file and then uploaded to website hope that's what you wanted ?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-29 8:17 p.m.
I'm starting to see the system here. Data flows through node red, gets pushed into a flat file
8:18 p.m.

I just implemented this. I'll be merging it to dev in the next couple days.

There's a lot of inconsistency in how high/low temps are formatted sometimes it's H:{today_high}{temp_symbol} L:{today_low}{temp_symbol} or {tomorrow_min}-{tomorrow_max}{temp_symbol} or {high}{temp_symbol}/{low}{temp_symbol} (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 12:20 a.m.
Working on this. Going to just make it user configurable with these defaults. # Placeholders: {high} {low} {units} ({units} is °F or °C) temperature_high_low_format = H:{high}{units} L:{low}{units} temperature_high_only_format = H:{high}{units} temperature_low_only_format = L:{low}{units} # Examples: # temperature_high_low_format = ↓{low}°↑{high}{units} # temperature_high_low_format = H:{high}{units} L:{low}{units} # temperature_high_low_format = {high}{units}/{low}{units}
Skye 2026-03-30 1:01 a.m.
Cool thanks! Configurable is even better
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 1:11 a.m.
That's how I end up with a config file that is like 3k lines long... but at least things work the way people want them to.
Skye 2026-03-30 1:42 a.m.
Haha yeah well it's well commented and I appreciated that

3:29 a.m.

Skye 2026-03-30 3:38 a.m.
Ah yup https://github.com/Kpa-clawbot/CoreScope/issues/276
Wrong TRACE packets parsing · Issue #276 · Kpa-clawbot/CoreScope
There appears to be a problem with TRACE packet processing (wrong path at minimum, 260001807dca00000000007d547d for example must be 7d-54-7d): Master branch build 30.03.2026. Here is a parsing of t...
3:42 a.m.
That's why the values kept changing on each trace, the SNR was changing....
3:42 a.m.
OK mystery solved, now I can sleep

Lïttlëaton 2026-03-30 9:08 a.m.
ok so like when you have a big update and add features, there are new config entries right? so i have to go in and manually add those or does the upgrade script append them in?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 9:13 a.m.
It does not currently append them. Most are set with what are (to me) sand defaults so you get the benefits for already enabled features. For brand new commands, they always start disabled and you can copy the block from config.ini.example to enable them. I try to list those in the release notes.
9:14 a.m.
Someday, I plan on making a config editor that is smarter, but it has been low-priority while feature requests roll in.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan
It does not currently append them. Most are set with what are (to me) sand defaults so you get the benefits for already enabled features. For brand new commands, they always start disabled and you can copy the block from config.ini.example to enable them. I try to list those in the release notes.

mnadler 2026-03-30 7:37 p.m.
i keep hitting Connection failed: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'is_connected'. I've checked that /dev/ttyACM0 is the USB connection to the companion node. I've tried both a manual installation and the production install. This is a fairly vanilla Linux Mint install. Is there some permission I need to update? Is there some way to determine if anything is coming in via the USB port?
mnadler
i keep hitting Connection failed: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'is_connected'. I've checked that /dev/ttyACM0 is the USB connection to the companion node. I've tried both a manual installation and the production install. This is a fairly vanilla Linux Mint install. Is there some permission I need to update? Is there some way to determine if anything is coming in via the USB port?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 7:46 p.m.
You may need to add whatever user you are running your bot as to the DIALOUT group in order to access serial ports. sudo usermod -a -G dialout <username> You can always cat < /dev/ttyACM0 to see if something is being emitted by the port.
mnadler 2026-03-30 7:47 p.m.
i'd tried the cat & nothing was emitted. in the production install, isn't the userid 'meshcore'? in my install that user is in the dialout group.

i'll add my uid for giggles
7:49 p.m.
still fails
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 7:56 p.m.

yes
7:57 p.m.
it sure seems like i have some perrmission incorrect
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 8:00 p.m.

James
Yes, that's the idea, normally I would create a file stream object and manipulate the format, but as a starter just displaying the line of text/data to the user would be cool !
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 8:45 p.m.
If you can drop the file in a place where your bot can access it (either via curl or as a file stream on the disk), I can help build a weather json parser that will work like the wxSim parser. The file format is flexible, and can be adapted to include the data you have available. You can kind of set the baseline for this, and we'll expand on it. For example, this is the OpenWeatherMap format: { "coord": { "lon": -122.08, "lat": 37.39 }, "weather": [ { "id": 800, "main": "Clear", "description": "clear sky", "icon": "01d" } ], "main": { "temp": 282.55, "feels_like": 281.86, "temp_min": 280.37, "temp_max": 284.26, "pressure": 1023, "humidity": 100 }, "visibility": 10000, "wind": { "speed": 1.5, "deg": 350 }, "clouds": { "all": 1 }, "dt": 1560350645, "name": "Mountain View", "cod": 200 } When the wxjson parser is added and the file is configured we'll set: custom.wxjson.<yourcity> = <file>

mnadler 2026-03-30 11:50 p.m.
so next question now that it's actually hearing stuff -- node_id should be auto detected. is the bot name what I use for hashtag channel name/
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 11:53 p.m.

mnadler 2026-03-30 11:53 p.m.
ok will try that
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-03-30 11:54 p.m.
Obviously adjust the values. The other thing is that those need to be on the radio to hear commands. If the web viewer is enabled, you can add them under the Radio tab. If not, connect to the radio at https://app.meshcore.nz/ and add them manually.
MeshCore
The MeshCore Companion App

[PNW] HerculesMulligan
If you can drop the file in a place where your bot can access it (either via curl or as a file stream on the disk), I can help build a weather json parser that will work like the wxSim parser. The file format is flexible, and can be adapted to include the data you have available. You can kind of set the baseline for this, and we'll expand on it. For example, this is the OpenWeatherMap format: { "coord": { "lon": -122.08, "lat": 37.39 }, "weather": [ { "id": 800, "main": "Clear", "description": "clear sky", "icon": "01d" } ], "main": { "temp": 282.55, "feels_like": 281.86, "temp_min": 280.37, "temp_max": 284.26, "pressure": 1023, "humidity": 100 }, "visibility": 10000, "wind": { "speed": 1.5, "deg": 350 }, "clouds": { "all": 1 }, "dt": 1560350645, "name": "Mountain View", "cod": 200 } When the wxjson parser is added and the file is configured we'll set: custom.wxjson.<yourcity> = <file>

James 2026-04-01 1:43 p.m.
msg.payload = JSON.parse(msg.payload); if (msg.payload.device == ("231")) { var newtemp = msg.payload.temperature_F - 32 ; newtemp = newtemp * 5 ; newtemp = newtemp / 9; newtemp = parseFloat(newtemp).toFixed(2); var hum = msg.payload.humidity; hum = hum - 0; var value ="Date/Time: " + msg.payload.time + "\nTemp: "+ newtemp + " deg.C " + "Humidity: " +hum +"%"; msg.payload = value; return msg; }

AEMON_GREMM 2026-04-02 10:18 a.m.
im not sure if this is a bot issue or just the way it is with 2byte. but a 1byte user does 'path' all the 2byte repeaters dont show up in the chain. (even as a 1byte relay). i assume that more of a repeater thing. (edited)
10:21 a.m.
if i send a path 2byte i see the 2byte paths, so i assume thats just the way it is with 1byte (edited)
AEMON_GREMM 2026-04-02 10:29 a.m.
i guess thats a good way to figure out who still has 1byte nodes up
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-02 10:35 a.m.
I have a neighboring repeater that is not updated and one that is. I see the one byte repeater in one-byte paths. My suspicion is that because the bot picks the fastest path,l (first to arrive) some of the multibyte repeaters are skipped. Would that track in your situation?
James 2026-04-02 2:53 p.m.
How do I know if I have a one byte repeater? The first byte is 44, but isn't that just the identifier ? I.e. when you do path command? Forgive my ignorance, I'm new at the Job Jay G8NAI
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-02 4:07 p.m.
If you have updated a repeater to 1.14.0 or later, it supports multibyte paths. When a user sends a packet with a one-byte path hash, you'll see 44. If they send a message with a two-byte path has, you'll see 44XX, and three-byte hashes will show 44XXXX.
1
AEMON_GREMM 2026-04-02 7:32 p.m.
its on 1.14.1

7:36 p.m.

7:36 p.m.

LittleTiTi 2026-04-03 9:32 a.m.
Hi, Is it possible to tag channels to bypass repeaters with region-based access? Can the bot identify the tags of everything it hears and create a list of them?
9:32 a.m.
Thanks by advence
LittleTiTi
Hi, Is it possible to tag channels to bypass repeaters with region-based access? Can the bot identify the tags of everything it hears and create a list of them?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-03 9:50 a.m.
Tell me a little more about this. What do you mean by "bypass"? The region hash is just a numerical string, I don't think we can work backward to a region without a list. Maybe there's a method I missed?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Tell me a little more about this. What do you mean by "bypass"? The region hash is just a numerical string, I don't think we can work backward to a region without a list. Maybe there's a method I missed?
LittleTiTi 2026-04-03 9:54 a.m.
The repeaters have region-based filtering. When the bot responds in #test, for example, how do I tag the response with the repeater's region name so that it repeats the frame?
LittleTiTi
The repeaters have region-based filtering. When the bot responds in #test, for example, how do I tag the response with the repeater's region name so that it repeats the frame?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-03 10:01 a.m.
Is this what you are looking for? # Flood scope for channel messages (MeshCore regions; optional) # Empty, * or 0: classic flood (default). Set to a region name (e.g. west) to limit # channel sends to that scope. Dynamic "reply with same scope as sender" is not # supported until the protocol exposes scope on received channel messages. # flood_scope =
1
James 2026-04-03 1:47 p.m.
How do I block someone using my bot (they keep sending path) I was sure i'd seen it in config, but not anymore?? update just found banned users, not sure what ID is but had a guess at T-1000 haha (edited)
James
How do I block someone using my bot (they keep sending path) I was sure i'd seen it in config, but not anymore?? update just found banned users, not sure what ID is but had a guess at T-1000 haha (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-03 2:08 p.m.
It will match names (since channel names aren't signed). It matches from the beginning of the string.
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
It will match names (since channel names aren't signed). It matches from the beginning of the string.
James 2026-04-03 3:41 p.m.
seems to be doing the trick thanks

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-03 11:29 p.m.
It's in the dev branch now. You'll need to copy two sections from config.ini.example — [MqttWeather] is under the Service Plugins section at the bottom, and there's some new settings under [Weather] toward the top to define your custom response keys (basically tell it what topic to look at for which key).
11:29 p.m.
I'm trying to kick a cold, so I'm headed to bed, but if you have questions tomorrow, I'll be around.

Daniel SP2EMW
The recent changes are a very good idea. I suggest extending channel protection to the #test channel. Let the bots work on #bot, #localbot, #local, etc. Let's keep the #test channel dedicated for human tests.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-10 5:02 a.m.

Daniel SP2EMW
The recent changes are a very good idea. I suggest extending channel protection to the #test channel. Let the bots work on #bot, #localbot, #local, etc. Let's keep the #test channel dedicated for human tests.

awhite 2026-04-10 9:19 a.m.
@[PNW] HerculesMulligan hmm, the latest dev doesn't start the webviewer. No errors, no logs. Also, which is strange, there's webviewer command in the admin_commands, and I have the proper public keys in admin_pubkeys. repeaters works but webviewer is rejected with permission denied
9:23 a.m.
I also noticed another weirdness with flood_scope / flood_scopes. If I set flood_scope = * and flood_scopes = waw, which is an existing scope, then it ignores any unscoped messages... Messages arrive and are captured but the bot doesn't respond.
awhite
I also noticed another weirdness with flood_scope / flood_scopes. If I set flood_scope = * and flood_scopes = waw, which is an existing scope, then it ignores any unscoped messages... Messages arrive and are captured but the bot doesn't respond.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-10 10:42 a.m.
I’ll look at the web viewer issue in a moment. Flood scope is the outgoing default. Flood scopes is the incoming allowed scopes. Are the test messages coming in unscoped? If so, you would need to add * as a permitted incoming scope.
Dodgy Andi Kirbi 2026-04-10 11:03 a.m.
Love your project... I have nothing else to add
2
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I’ll look at the web viewer issue in a moment. Flood scope is the outgoing default. Flood scopes is the incoming allowed scopes. Are the test messages coming in unscoped? If so, you would need to add * as a permitted incoming scope.
awhite 2026-04-10 11:43 a.m.
ahhh, makes sense - I'll try it in a moment
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-10 11:45 a.m.
You may need to set a web_viewer password if you have the host set to 0.0.0.0. web_viewer_password = <password> # Web viewer host address # 127.0.0.1: Only accessible from localhost # 0.0.0.0: Accessible from any network interface host = 0.0.0.0 (edited)
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
You may need to set a web_viewer password if you have the host set to 0.0.0.0. web_viewer_password = <password> # Web viewer host address # 127.0.0.1: Only accessible from localhost # 0.0.0.0: Accessible from any network interface host = 0.0.0.0 (edited)

hmm flod_scopes kinda works but not quite... I defined the following: flood_scope = * flood_scopes = #waw,* (edited)
12:09 p.m.
now, sending a packet without scope properly produces response without scope, but sending a packet with scope #waw produces response without scope, too
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-10 12:11 p.m.
Yes, if you comment out flood_scope = * then it will just engage in matching behavior. That's why I'm going to rename the flood_scope setting outgoing_flood_scope_override
awhite 2026-04-10 12:12 p.m.
ahhh, ok
12:12 p.m.
let me try it again
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-10 12:13 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-16 4:59 p.m.
I'm struggling to reproduce this, or find a strong pattern. I checked the 11 bots that are feeding the Cascadia Mesh CoreScope analyzer, and only one is exhibiting this behavior. I suspect that it might be a meshcore_py library issue, because the bot just takes the raw packet value and writes it into the json that goes to the MQTT server. Then CoreScope is parsing that raw value off the radio. Can you help me out with a test # Activate venv first if needed: source .venv/bin/activate pip show meshcore and then maybe: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print(m.version('meshcore'))" (edited)

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-04-21 10:27 p.m.
Okay, I think I figured out why it was variable. There was a double-write for packets under certain circumstances, and the second path had an issue. I think I've fixed it in dev.
10:28 p.m.
I had to go back to my meshcore-packet-capture project as a reference and realized I'd missed a trick when correlating packets that sometimes resulted in the fallback path running. Now I've basically removed the fallback, because we'd rather fail than corrupt data.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
Okay, I think I figured out why it was variable. There was a double-write for packets under certain circumstances, and the second path had an issue. I think I've fixed it in dev.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan
There are lots of new features in dev, but your existing config should get you a functional bot with conservative defaults for everything else.
